plan.action: Audit existing clean.sh and cli.bats: verify clean_run, clean_checkout_main, clean_discard_changes, clean_delete_branch, clean_remove_lock are all implemented and unit-tested
Add CLI integration tests to tests/cli.bats covering: --clean exits 0, --clean checks out main, --clean removes LOCK file, --clean --force discards uncommitted changes, --clean on non-git dir exits 1
Run shellcheck (typecheck) across clippy.sh and all lib/*.sh files to ensure no typecheck errors are introduced; fix any findings
Run full test suite (bats tests/) to confirm all existing tests still pass and new CLI tests pass
Mark US-023 passes=true in the PRD file

testing_recommendations: Add --clean integration tests to tests/cli.bats: --clean exits 0 on clean repo, --clean checks out main branch, --clean removes LOCK file, --clean on non-git workspace exits 1 with ERROR message
Add --force integration test to tests/cli.bats: --clean --force discards uncommitted changes and still exits 0
Verify warning message is printed when --clean is used without --force on a dirty tree
Ensure clean.bats unit tests all still pass (26 tests currently passing)
Run shellcheck on clippy.sh and lib/clean.sh to validate typecheck
risks: cli.bats setup creates a valid workspace; --clean tests must NOT call workspace_init (clean runs before it) — use a minimal git-only setup for --clean test cases to avoid workspace validation failures
The --clean path in clippy.sh calls git_is_repo before clean_run; cli.bats tests for --clean must set up a git repo or they will exit 1 with a different error than expected
